name: Al-Wehdat SC
description: One of Jordan's most popular football clubs, founded in 1956, based in the Al-Wehdat Palestinian refugee camp in Amman. Competes in the Jordanian Pro League. Home games at King Abdullah II Stadium.

name: Al-Faisaly SC
description: One of Jordan's oldest and most successful football clubs, a founding member of the Jordanian Pro League. Holds the record for most Jordan FA Cup wins (11). Home games at Amman International Stadium.

name: Al-Ahli SC Amman
description: Founded in 1944, one of Jordan's oldest sports clubs. Originally founded under the Circassians name Koban Club. Plays home games at Amman International Stadium. Kit: all white with green away kit.

name: Al-Hussein SC Irbid
description: Professional football club based in Irbid, Jordan. Three-time consecutive Jordanian Pro League champion (2023-24, 2024-25, 2025-26). One of the top clubs in the country.

name: Al-Ramtha SC
description: Professional football club based in Ar Ramtha, Jordan. Competes in the Jordanian Pro League and consistently ranks among the top 5 national clubs.

name: Al-Jazeera SC Amman
description: Jordanian multisports club based in Amman, founded in 1947. Plays at Amman International Stadium. Home kit: all red shirts and white shorts.

name: Al-Baqa'a SC
description: Professional football club based in Ain Al-Basha District, Balqa Governorate. Formed in 1968 and based in the Baqa'a refugee camp. Competes in the Jordanian Pro League.

name: Shabab Al-Ordon SC
description: Jordanian professional football club based in Amman, established in 2002. Has won two league titles (2006 and 2007). Home ground is the Amman International Stadium.

name: Ittihad Al-Ramtha SC
description: Jordanian football club based in Ar-Ramtha. Competes in the Jordanian First Division League, the second tier of Jordanian football.

name: Al-Salt SC
description: Professional football club based in Al-Salt, Jordan. Competes in Jordanian football competitions.

name: Al-Sareeh SC
description: Jordanian football club competing in the national football league system.

name: Aqaba SC
description: Football club based in Aqaba, Jordan's only coastal city. Competes in Jordanian football league competitions.

name: Jordan Football Association
description: The governing body of football in Jordan, responsible for organizing and overseeing all football competitions including the Jordanian Pro League, FA Cup, and national team.

name: Jordan Basketball Federation
description: Governing body of basketball in Jordan, founded in 1957. Affiliated with FIBA and FIBA Asia. Organizes the Jordanian Premier Basketball League.

name: Jordan Volleyball Federation
description: Governing body of volleyball in Jordan, established in 1961. Volleyball was first introduced to Jordan in 1938.

name: Jordan Olympic Committee
description: The national Olympic committee of Jordan, overseeing all Olympic sports federations and representing Jordan in international Olympic competitions.

name: Amman International Stadium
description: Located in Al-Hussein City, Amman. Built in 1964, opened in 1968. Home of the Jordan national football team, Al-Faisaly SC, Al-Jazeera, and Al-Baqa'a. Capacity of 17,619 spectators.

name: King Abdullah II Stadium
description: Football stadium in Amman, Jordan. Home ground of Al-Wehdat SC. Capacity of 13,265 seats.

name: Al Hussein Youth City
description: Jordan's first sports city, a major sports complex in Amman featuring multiple sports facilities including stadiums, courts, and recreational areas for various sports.

name: City Mall Amman
description: Major shopping mall in Amman opened in 2006, owned by Al-Khayr Real Estate Investment Company. Extends over 160,000 m² with 55,000 m² leasable area. Anchors include Carrefour, Zara, H&M, Debenhams, Grand Cinemas IMAX, Jingo's Jungle, TGI Fridays.

name: Mecca Mall
description: One of the largest shopping centers in Jordan, located on Mecca Street in Amman. Covers 65,000 square meters with over 450 well-known brands. Features gaming zone, cinema, and children's play areas.

name: Abdali Mall
description: Modern mall in the heart of Amman's Abdali district with international and local brands. Features a 9-screen Cineplex with Jordan's largest IMAX theater and a family entertainment center. Mix of luxury retail, premium fashion, and gourmet dining.

name: Taj Mall
description: Luxury shopping mall in the upscale Abdoun district of Amman. Spans 60,000 square meters with high-end brands like Louis Vuitton and Burberry. Features rooftop terraces with views of Amman and diverse dining options.

name: Al Baraka Mall
description: Shopping mall in Abdoun, Amman, combining luxury shopping with various facilities. Houses local and international brands, restaurants, cafes, and a cinema across multiple levels.

name: Irbid Mall
description: Premier shopping destination in Irbid with over 150 stores ranging from international brands to local boutiques. Offers dining options and entertainment including a cinema.

name: Irbid City Centre
description: The first and biggest shopping centre in the northern part of Jordan, located in Irbid City. Features cinemas, children's play areas, and a wide range of retail stores.

name: Arabella Mall Irbid
description: Shopping mall in Irbid with coffee shops and stores for gifts, accessories, handbags, and cosmetics. Serves as a local retail and social hub.

name: Grand Cinemas Jordan
description: The fastest growing cinema chain in the Levant. Located at City Mall in Amman. Features the only IMAX theatre in Jordan. Phone: +962 6 581 8808.

name: Prime Cinemas Jordan
description: Cinema chain operating in Jordan with online ticketing available through prime.jo. One of the major movie theater operators in the country.

name: Cine Le Royal
description: Cinema located at Le Royal Hotel in Amman with 3 screens and 495 seats. Phone: +962 460 3022.

name: Grand City Mall Cinema
description: Cinema located at Grand City Mall on Medical City Street in Amman. Part of the Grand Cinemas chain offering the latest movie releases.

name: The Jordan Museum
description: The largest museum in Jordan, opened in 2014. Presents the history and cultural heritage of Jordan from the Paleolithic era to modern times through three major galleries: archaeology, folklore, and contemporary history.

name: Jordan Archaeological Museum
description: Built in 1951 at the Amman Citadel, houses over 2,000 artifacts from numerous archaeological sites including the 8,700-year-old Ain Ghazal Statues, Nabataean exhibits, and Dead Sea Scrolls. Entry included with Amman Citadel ticket (3 JOD).

name: Royal Automobile Museum
description: The first public automotive museum in the Arab region, inaugurated by King Abdullah II in 2003 as a tribute to the late King Hussein. Displays 80 of King Hussein's cars and motorcycles, some dating to 1916. Located in Al Hussein Public Parks.

name: Children's Museum Jordan
description: The first interactive children's museum in Jordan, opened in 2007 by Queen Rania Al Abdullah. Features over 150 indoor and outdoor exhibits. Located in Al Hussein Public Parks alongside the Royal Automobile Museum.

name: Jordan Folklore Museum
description: Established in 1974 on the eastern side of the Roman Theater in Amman. Collects, preserves, researches, and records Jordanian traditional heritage. Run by the Department of Antiquities.

name: Numismatic Museum - Central Bank of Jordan
description: A coinage and numismatics museum established by the Central Bank of Jordan. Features over 4,500 coins dating back to 600 B.C., including Nabataean silver and copper and Umayyad copper coin collections.

name: Prophet Muhammad Museum
description: Opened in 2012, located in the King Hussein Mosque in al-Hussein Gardens. Includes artifacts connected to the Prophet Muhammad, such as his letter to the Byzantine emperor Heraclius.

name: Jordan National Gallery of Fine Arts
description: Established in 1980, a hub for contemporary art and cultural exchange. Features diverse collections of paintings, sculptures, multimedia, photography, and weaving from around the world. Hosts lectures, seminars, workshops, a sculpture park, and a rooftop café.

name: Darat Al Funun
description: The most important Jordanian art center, opened in 1988. Located in six historical 19th-century villas in Jabal Al Weibdeh with an archaeological site in the garden. Established to bring together and support artists from Jordan and the wider Arab world.

name: Nabad Art Gallery
description: Contemporary art gallery in Jabal Amman, dedicated to Middle Eastern art. Housed in a renovated 1930s residence with panoramic views of downtown Amman. Hosts exhibitions, art talks, workshops, and musical events.

name: Wadi Finan Gallery
description: Established in 2008 under the patronage of the Mayor of Amman. Located in Jabal Amman. Known for multimedia works, arts education, and community collaborations including art therapy workshops for refugees and people with disabilities.

name: Nofa Creative Space
description: Creative space located in a nearly century-old house in Amman. Features art exhibitions, concerts, and lectures. Also used for personal and corporate events.

name: Royal Cultural Centre
description: Education and cultural institution established in 1983 under King Hussein, continuing under King Abdullah II. Located in Shmeisani. Houses the Royal Theatre (300 seats), Conference Hall (30,000 sqm), Auditorium, Studio Theater, and Exhibition Hall. Hosts theater, dance, music, and cultural events.

name: Royal Film Commission Jordan
description: Public organization founded in 2003 under HRH Prince Ali Bin Al Hussein. Promotes film culture, provides educational programs, supports local and foreign productions, and positions Jordan as an international film destination. Housed in the historic Mango House.

name: National Music Conservatory
description: Jordan's first music academy, established in 1985-1986 by Queen Noor through the Noor Al Hussein Foundation. Develops accomplished musicians in classical Arabic and Western music and promotes music appreciation in Jordan.

name: Amman Institute of Performing Arts (AIPA)
description: Arts institute in Jordan providing training in music and drama, catering to aspiring professionals and keen amateurs.

name: Odeon Theater Amman
description: 2nd-century Roman theater located adjacent to the larger Roman Theater in downtown Amman. Capacity of about 500 seats in 9 rows. Regularly used for performances during the Al Balad Music Festival. Recently restored.

name: Jingo's Jungle
description: Jordan's largest themed indoor family entertainment center, located at City Mall on the third floor. Features hundreds of rides, side show games, and computer games for all age groups.

name: Lollihop Jordan
description: One of Amman's newest and largest indoor playground and entertainment centers. Over 2,000 square meters featuring indoor playground, volcano slides, trampoline jumping, and shooting ball guns.

name: Tara Park
description: Children's indoor play area in Amman's Dabouq District with thrilling slides and a separate toddler area for younger children.

name: Al Hussein Public Parks (Luna Park)
description: Major public park complex in Amman spanning approximately 70 acres. Features Luna Park with rides and amusements including Ferris wheels, teacup rides and pedalos. Also houses the Royal Automobile Museum, Children's Museum, and King Hussein Mosque.

name: King Hussein Park
description: Prominent public green space in Amman dedicated to the late King Hussein. Established in 2006. Features a 488-meter history corridor with murals, a cultural village, amphitheater, and 8.5 km of pathways. Open daily 6AM-9PM, 1 JOD entry.

name: Jordan Speed Center
description: Go-karting and entertainment venue near Queen Alia Airport Road. Features indoor activities including mini golf, escape games, and VR games in addition to go-karting.

name: VRCADE Amman
description: Virtual reality gaming center located at Mecca Mall Street in Amman. Open Monday to Sunday from 11am to 12:30am. Offers immersive VR experiences with cutting-edge technology.

name: The Escape Jordan
description: Amman's premier escape room experience. Players are locked in a themed room with 60 minutes to solve puzzles and find hidden clues. One of the top escape room venues in Jordan.

name: Diamond Bowling Amman
description: Popular bowling venue in Amman. Offers corporate events, birthday celebrations, and dining options.

name: Strikers Entertainment Center
description: Entertainment center in Amman offering 8 bowling lanes plus table tennis and pool tables, with food options including burgers and pizza.

name: La Cueva Aqua Park
description: Leading water entertainment destination located on the Dead Sea coast. Features over 26 water attractions for all ages. Caters to families, schools, corporate events, and private celebrations.

name: Al-Wadi Water Park
description: Water park on the shores of the Dead Sea. Features a wave pool, lazy river, kiddie pool, multilevel play structure, 4-lane multislide, family raft ride, Master Blaster water coaster, Space Bowl, and Kamikaze speed slide.

name: Saraya Aqaba Waterpark
description: Jordan's largest aqua park, located in Aqaba on the Red Sea coast. Features over 25 rides and attractions inspired by Jordan's iconic landmarks including Wadi Rum, the Dead Sea, and Jerash. Family-friendly dining also available.

name: Dana Biosphere Reserve
description: Jordan's largest biosphere reserve, established in 1989 and recognized by UNESCO in 1998. Covers 320 square kilometers encompassing mountains, valleys, and deserts. Features two major biogeographical zones and four vegetation zones with dramatic 1,600-meter elevation drop.

name: Ajloun Forest Reserve
description: Nature reserve in northwestern Jordan covering 13 square kilometers, established in 1988. Known for oak and pine forests. Haven for species including Persian deer.

name: Wadi Rum Protected Area
description: UNESCO World Heritage protected area famous for its vast red rock and sand landscapes, towering red dunes, unique rock formations, and ancient inscriptions. A premier adventure destination offering camping, jeep tours, and rock climbing.

name: Wadi Mujib Nature Reserve
description: Nature reserve managed by the RSCN, known as the 'Grand Canyon of Jordan.' Offers canyoning, hiking, and water activities in the dramatic gorges flowing into the Dead Sea.

name: Azraq Wetland Reserve
description: Wetland nature reserve in the Azraq Oasis managed by the RSCN. Important stopover for migratory birds. One of Jordan's seven protected areas.

name: Shaumari Wildlife Reserve
description: Wildlife reserve managed by the RSCN, established for the reintroduction of endangered species including the Arabian oryx. One of Jordan's seven protected areas.

name: Dibeen Forest Reserve
description: Pine forest reserve in northern Jordan managed by the RSCN. One of the last remaining natural pine forests in Jordan. Offers hiking, picnicking and wildlife observation.

name: Petra Archaeological Site
description: One of the Seven Wonders of the World. The Nabataean city carved into rose-red rock, founded in the 5th-6th centuries BC. Key sites include Al-Khazneh (The Treasury), the Monastery, the Royal Tombs, Roman Theater, and the High Place of Sacrifice.

name: Jerash Archaeological Site
description: Jordan's second most visited site after Petra. One of the world's best preserved ancient Roman cities. Features the Temple of Artemis, the Forum with ionic columns, the Cardo (main street with chariot marks), a 3,000-seat South Theatre, and a Nymphaeum fountain.

name: Umm Qais (Gadara)
description: Ruins of the ancient Greco-Roman city of Gadara, one of the Decapolis cities. Features remains of a theater, churches, shops, nymphaeum, baths, and paved roads. Offers unique panoramic views of the Sea of Galilee, Golan Heights, and Yarmouk Valley.

name: Madaba Archaeological Park
description: Archaeological park in Madaba displaying various churches with colorful mosaics of animals, plants, and mythological creatures. Home to the famous Madaba Map at St George's Church—the oldest surviving mosaic map of the Holy Land.

name: Aqaba Ayla Archaeological Site
description: The early Islamic city of Ayla, built around 650 AD. Notable as the first Islamic city built outside the Arabian Peninsula. Served as a port and stopping place for pilgrims traveling to Mecca.

name: Four Seasons Hotel Amman
description: Luxury five-star hotel in Amman. Features the Four Seasons Spa offering luxury massages, facial treatments, body treatments, and salon services. One of Amman's premier luxury properties.

name: The Ritz-Carlton Amman
description: Luxury five-star hotel in Amman, offering premium accommodations, dining, spa, and event facilities. One of the most prestigious hotels in the country.

name: Fairmont Amman
description: Luxury five-star hotel in Amman with over 4,000 square meters of versatile event spaces, including a 684 sqm ballroom accommodating up to 480 sit-down diners or 600 cocktail guests.

name: InterContinental Jordan Hotel Amman
description: Iconic five-star hotel located atop one of Amman's seven hills. Offers a Grand Ballroom for meetings and weddings, seven smaller meeting rooms, and a blend of cosmopolitan amenities with Middle Eastern charm.

name: Grand Hyatt Amman
description: Five-star luxury hotel in Amman offering flexible event spaces including a magnificent ballroom with custom catering and expert event management.

name: W Amman
description: Luxury lifestyle hotel in Amman from the W Hotels brand. Features contemporary design, nightlife, dining, and premium accommodations.

name: Sheraton Amman Al Nabil Hotel
description: Five-star Sheraton property in Amman offering premium accommodations, dining, and business facilities.

name: Crowne Plaza Amman
description: Five-star IHG hotel in Amman with five impressive venues including an opulent ballroom for meetings, weddings, and events.

name: Movenpick Hotel Amman
description: Well-established hotel in Amman with 9 event spaces configurable for weddings and business meetings, diverse dining, and comfortable accommodations.

name: Le Royal Hotels & Resorts Amman
description: Luxury five-star hotel in Amman featuring premier meeting rooms, state-of-the-art facilities for conferences, and outdoor and indoor wedding packages.

name: Amman Marriott Hotel
description: Five-star Marriott property in Amman, part of the global Marriott hotel chain offering premium accommodations and services.

name: Kempinski Hotel Ishtar Dead Sea
description: Luxurious five-star resort on the Dead Sea. Features Ishtar Spa by Resense, reportedly the region's largest spa at roughly 10,000 sqm with 20 treatment rooms, six outdoor treatment areas, and the largest hydro-pool in the area.

name: Movenpick Resort & Spa Dead Sea
description: Five-star resort at the Dead Sea featuring Zara Spa, one of the Middle East's most sophisticated wellness destinations spanning 6,000 sqm with hydrotherapy pools, a Dead Sea pool, steam rooms, tropical showers, and a traditional Hammam.

name: Crowne Plaza Jordan Dead Sea Resort & Spa
name_ar: منتجع وسبا كراون بلازا الأردن البحر الميت
description: Luxury five-star Dead Sea resort by IHG offering spa treatments, recreational facilities, and beautiful Dead Sea views.

name: Dead Sea Marriott Resort & Spa
description: Five-star Marriott resort on the shores of the Dead Sea, offering spa services, pools, and full resort amenities.

name: InterContinental Aqaba
description: Five-star hotel in Aqaba on the Red Sea coast, offering beach access, water sports, dining, and leisure facilities.

name: Hyatt Regency Aqaba Ayla Resort
description: Exclusive five-star resort in Aqaba offering luxurious accommodations with Red Sea views and resort amenities.

name: Kempinski Hotel Aqaba
description: Modern and stylish five-star hotel in Aqaba with a private beach on the Red Sea. Offers luxury accommodations and water sports activities.

name: Movenpick Resort & Residences Aqaba
description: Five-star resort and residences in Aqaba offering Red Sea views, beach access, dining, and full resort facilities.

name: Al Manara Luxury Collection Hotel Aqaba
description: Five-star luxury hotel in Aqaba, part of the Marriott Luxury Collection. Offers premium beachfront accommodations on the Red Sea.

name: Movenpick Resort Petra
description: Resort hotel in Petra/Wadi Musa area offering comfortable accommodations near the world-famous archaeological site.

name: Petra Marriott Hotel
description: Five-star Marriott hotel near the Petra archaeological site, offering premium accommodations with stunning views of the surrounding valley.

name: Centro Mada Amman by Rotana
description: Four-star hotel in Amman by Rotana consistently meeting high expectations with modern amenities and quality service.

name: Papillon Venue
description: One of Amman's premier destinations for weddings, corporate events, and private gatherings. Offers elegant event spaces with professional event management.

name: Ma'in Hot Springs Resort & Spa
description: Unique natural hot springs spa resort. Mineral-rich water heated by geothermal activity emerges at 45-60°C cascading down waterfalls. Features a Six Senses Spa offering luxury wellness treatments combined with natural hot spring experiences.

name: Elixir Day Spa Amman
description: Full-range professional spa in Amman offering state-of-the-art facilities and professional beauty treatments. Award-winning spa known for its quality services.

name: Guinot Spa Amman
description: Premium spa in Amman offering Moroccan Hammam and Technispa treatments. Highly rated for its specialized European beauty treatments.

name: Gaia's Cocoon Spa
description: Diverse spa in Amman offering treatments from Thai massage to Turkish and Moroccan Hammams. Features a hair salon, manicure and pedicure salon, hair removal, facials, masks, henna art, body toning, and nutrition consultations.

name: TONI&GUY Jordan
description: Premier hair salon chain in Amman, part of the global TONI&GUY franchise with over 485 salons worldwide and 55 years of hairdressing excellence. Known for precision, professionalism, and style.

name: Gold's Gym Jordan
description: The world's largest Gold's Gym, opened in Amman in 2017. 14,000 sqm across six floors featuring four swimming pools, Kid's Club, multiple exercise studios (Mind & Body, Cycling, GGX, Boxing), Outdoor Gym, Functional Fitness, and three floors of ladies' training zones. Won 'Best New International Gym, Vegas 2017'.

name: Hive Gym
description: Known as the largest gym in Jordan with dynamic classes, qualified coaches, and a strong mental wellness focus. Offers energy, community, and good vibes for all fitness levels.

name: Magma Wellness Center
description: Full wellness destination in Amman offering group classes for all ages, personalized training, nutrition consultations, meal plans, recovery services, and even DNA testing.

name: Rizeup Gym
description: Located in King Hussein Business Park in Amman. Massive high-tech fitness space featuring cardio machines, Les Mills studios, MMA training, squash courts, and pools.

name: Vy Health Club
description: Luxury fitness destination in Amman blending luxury with performance. Features energizing group classes, personalized training, holistic wellness support, a semi-Olympic pool, and luxe recovery facilities.

name: Abdul Hameed Shoman Public Library
description: Major cultural and educational hub in Amman with a vast collection of books, periodicals, and digital resources. Regularly hosts cultural events, lectures, and workshops. One of the most prominent public libraries in Jordan.

name: National Library of Jordan
description: Comprehensive library preserving Jordan's national documents and publications. Provides access to books, manuscripts, and periodicals. The national archive of Jordanian literary and intellectual heritage.

name: British Council Library Amman
description: Library in Amman offering a wide range of English-language books, educational resources, and cultural programs. Serves as a bridge for cultural exchange between the UK and Jordan.

name: Books@Cafe Amman
description: Unique combination bookstore and café in Amman offering a cozy atmosphere. Known for its selection of English-language books. A beloved cultural gathering spot.

name: Readers Bookshop Amman
description: Popular bookshop in Amman with a wide range of books in both English and Arabic. Hosts book signings and cultural events. A community hub for book lovers.

name: University Bookshop Amman
description: The most pioneering bookshop in Amman since 1969. Offers books on a wide range of topics including politics, engineering, and self-help, as well as bestsellers.

name: The Good Book Shop
description: Cozy bookshop located on Rainbow Street in Amman. Known for its welcoming atmosphere, good coffee, and diverse selection including bestsellers, classics, and children's books.

name: Azbakeya Amman
description: Bookstore in Jabal Amman with history dating to 2012. Offers fiction, history, art, and science books at remarkably affordable prices. Named after the famous book market in Cairo.

name: Mahall Al-Maa (Water Bookstore)
description: Unique bookstore on Quraysh Street near the Roman Nymphaeum in downtown Amman. Operates with the motto 'take a book and read, pay any price you please.' A community-oriented cultural landmark.

name: Deep Blue Dive Center Aqaba
description: Professional PADI diving center in Aqaba offering guided dives, equipment rental, certification courses, and boat trips to Red Sea dive sites. Access to 21 dive sites including Cedar Pride Wreck and Japanese Garden.

name: Aqaba Adventure Divers
description: PADI diving and snorkeling center in Aqaba. Offers snorkeling and scuba diving experiences in the Red Sea with guides for all experience levels.

name: Coral Garden Diving Center Aqaba
description: Diving center in Aqaba offering scuba diving experiences in the Red Sea. Features access to healthy coral reefs with over 200 species of hard and soft corals.

name: Aqaba Marine Park
description: Protected marine area in Aqaba with 21 dive sites, 14 reachable from the shore. Features exceptional water clarity (35-40 meters average), healthy coral reefs, and diverse marine life. Water temperature 21-27°C depending on season.

name: Arthur Murray Jordan Dance School
name_ar: مدرسة آرثر موراي للرقص في الأردن
description: Ballroom and Latin dance school operating in Amman. Offers various dance styles including waltz, tango, salsa, and other ballroom and Latin dances for all ages and skill levels.

name: Amman Chamber Orchestra
description: Classical music orchestra based in Amman, Jordan. Performs concerts at major venues including the Royal Cultural Centre. Contributes to the cultural and musical life of the capital.

name: Cinema Squad Amman
description: The first and only home movie theater rental experience in Jordan. Provides private cinema room rentals for personal events and screenings.

name: Roman Theater Amman
description: Impressive 2nd-century Roman theater in the heart of downtown Amman. Carved into a hillside with a capacity of 6,000 seats, still used for cultural performances and events. One of Amman's most iconic landmarks.

name: Amman Citadel
description: Historical site on the highest hill in Amman containing ruins from various civilizations. Major remains include the Temple of Hercules, a Byzantine church, and the Umayyad Palace. Houses the Jordan Archaeological Museum. Entry: 3 JOD.

name: Jubaiha Recreational City
description: One of the largest entertainment cities in Jordan, located in Jubaiha, Amman. Offers modern games and rides suitable for children and adults alike.

name: King Hussein Foundation
description: Founded by Queen Noor in 1979. An umbrella organization including the Noor Al Hussein Foundation and eight specialized development institutions: the Jubilee Institute, the Information and Research Center, the National Music Conservatory, the National Center for Culture and Arts, and the Institute for Family Health.

name: Royal Society for the Conservation of Nature (RSCN)
description: Jordan's primary nature conservation organization managing seven protected areas: Dana, Wadi Mujib, Azraq, Shaumari, Dibeen, Ajloun, and Wadi Rum. Promotes eco-tourism and wildlife conservation.

name: Jerash Festival for Culture and Arts
description: Annual cultural festival held at the Jerash archaeological site attracting over 100,000 visitors. Celebrates Jordanian and Arab culture through music, dance, theater, and visual arts performances in the ancient Roman city.

name: Aqaba Gateway
description: Shopping and dining destination in Aqaba with diverse restaurants, cafes, and small shops. Popular leisure spot for residents and tourists in Jordan's coastal city.

name: FC Ma'an
description: Football club based in Ma'an, Jordan, competing in the national football league structure. Represents the Ma'an Governorate in Jordanian football competitions.

name: Al-Jalil SC
description: Jordanian football club competing in the national football league system. Part of the broad network of Jordanian professional and semi-professional football clubs.

name: Mansheyat Bani Hasan SC
description: Jordanian football club competing in the national league structure, representing the Manshiyeh area.

name: Etihad Club Jordan
description: Jordanian football club competing in the national football league competitions.

name: Sukar Mall Amman
description: Shopping center in the Gardens area of Amman featuring various shops, cafes, and entertainment facilities for families.

name: Red Sea Dive Center Aqaba
description: Diving center in Aqaba providing access to Red Sea dive sites. Offers certification courses and guided dives for all experience levels.

name: Amman Citadel Museum (Amman Museum)
description: Museum at the Amman Citadel site showcasing finds from the Citadel excavations. Features Bronze Age artifacts, Iron Age pottery, and other finds from the Amman area's rich archaeological history.

name: Center for the Study of the Built Environment (CSBE)
description: Jordanian cultural and research institution dedicated to architecture, urban planning, and built heritage. Maintains a directory of Jordan's museums, galleries, art foundations, and cultural centers.

name: Al Hussein Public Parks Entertainment Zone
name_ar: منطقة الترفيه في حدائق الحسين العامة
description: Entertainment complex within Al Hussein Public Parks in Amman, featuring the Royal Automobile Museum, Children's Museum Jordan, and the King Hussein Mosque alongside recreational areas and green spaces.

name: Escape The Room Jordan
description: Escape room entertainment venue in Amman offering themed puzzle rooms. Players must solve clues and challenges within a time limit.

name: Zara Spa Dead Sea
description: One of the Middle East's most sophisticated wellness destinations at Mövenpick Resort & Spa Dead Sea. Spans 6,000 sqm with hydrotherapy pools, a Dead Sea pool, steam rooms, tropical showers, a traditional Hammam sanctuary, and an exclusive VIP Royal Therapy Suite.

name: Ishtar Spa by Resense
description: Reportedly the region's largest spa at Kempinski Hotel Ishtar Dead Sea. Approximately 10,000 sqm with 20 treatment rooms, six outdoor treatment areas, and the largest hydro-pool in the Dead Sea cluster.

name: Four Seasons Spa Amman
description: Luxury hotel spa at Four Seasons Hotel Amman offering massages, facial treatments, body treatments, and full salon services. One of Amman's top luxury wellness destinations.

name: Petra Visitor Center
description: Official visitor center for the Petra Archaeological Park. Provides information, maps, guides, and services for tourists visiting the Nabataean city of Petra.

name: Ajloun Castle (Qal'at Ar-Rabad)
description: 12th-century Islamic castle built by the Ayyubid dynasty in Ajloun. Offers stunning views of the northern Jordan Valley and surrounding forests. One of Jordan's most visited historical sites.

name: Karak Castle
description: Crusader-era castle in Karak, one of the largest crusader castles in the Levant. Features dramatic views and a museum with artifacts from the Crusader and Mamluk periods.

name: Shobak Castle
description: 12th-century Crusader castle in Shobak, south of Amman near Petra. Known as Montreal, one of the first Crusader castles built in the region. Features underground tunnels and panoramic views.

name: Madaba St George's Church and Map
description: Greek Orthodox church in Madaba housing the famous 6th-century Madaba Map—the oldest surviving mosaic of the Holy Land. A major religious tourism attraction drawing pilgrims and history enthusiasts.

name: Mount Nebo
description: Sacred site overlooking the Jordan Valley where Moses is said to have viewed the Promised Land. Features Byzantine mosaics, the Moses Memorial Church, and panoramic views of the Dead Sea, Jericho, and Jerusalem on clear days.

name: Baptism Site of Jesus (Al-Maghtas)
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site on the eastern bank of the Jordan River, recognized as the site of Jesus' baptism. Features Byzantine and early Christian churches and a major pilgrimage destination.

name: Wadi Rum Visitor Center
description: Official visitor center and entry point to the Wadi Rum Protected Area. Organizes permits, jeep tours, camel rides, and camping arrangements for visitors to the protected desert landscape.

name: Umm Ar-Rasas Archaeological Park
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site south of Madaba containing remains of the Roman Kastron Mefa'a. Features exceptionally well-preserved Byzantine and Umayyad mosaic floors.

name: Azraq Castle
description: Roman-era castle in Azraq, notable as the winter headquarters of T.E. Lawrence (Lawrence of Arabia) during the Arab Revolt. Built of black basalt stone.

name: Dead Sea Shoreline
description: The lowest point on earth at approximately 430m below sea level. Famous for its hypersaline waters (34% salt concentration) and mineral-rich black mud. A major wellness and tourism destination with multiple resorts.

name: Amman Design Week
description: Annual design festival in Amman celebrating creativity and design culture across architecture, fashion, art, food, and urban innovation. One of the region's key creative events.

name: Al Balad Theater
description: Cultural theater in downtown Amman that launched the Al Balad Music Festival in 2009. Supported by the Greater Amman Municipality, Ministry of Tourism, and European Union. Hosts music, poetry readings, debates, and lectures.

name: Power Hut The Xperience
description: Fitness center and gym in Amman offering a range of workout options and fitness classes in a dynamic environment.

name: QuickSand Gym
description: Popular gym in Amman offering training programs and fitness classes. Well-regarded by the local fitness community.

name: Wellness Way Amman
description: Wellness and fitness center in Amman offering a holistic approach to health including fitness training and wellness programs.

name: Jordan Speed Polo Club
description: Polo sports club near Amman operating at the Polo Stadium. Part of the sports infrastructure at Al Hussein Youth City complex.

name: Aqaba Bird Observatory
description: Bird watching observatory in Aqaba, a key stopover point along major bird migration routes between Africa and Eurasia. Important site for ornithologists and eco-tourists.

name: Sweimah Dead Sea Beach Resorts Area
name_ar: منطقة منتجعات شاطئ سويمة البحر الميت
description: Premier Dead Sea resort area in Sweimah with multiple five-star hotels and spas. Known for therapeutic mud treatments, floating in hypersaline waters, and luxury wellness experiences.

name: Jordan Museum of Natural History
description: Natural history museum in Irbid at the Yarmouk University campus. Displays Jordan's natural heritage including geological, biological, and zoological collections.

name: Dar Al-Anda Art Gallery
description: Art gallery in Amman showcasing works by Jordanian and regional artists, contributing to the vibrant contemporary art scene in the city.

name: Haya Cultural Centre
description: Cultural center in Amman providing arts education, activities, and cultural programming for children and families.

name: Luxotel Aqaba Beach Resort & Spa
description: Four-star beach resort and spa in Aqaba offering comfortable accommodations with Red Sea views and spa facilities.

name: Jordan Bike Trail
description: Long-distance cycling route running the length of Jordan from Umm Qais in the north to Aqaba in the south. Showcases Jordan's diverse landscapes and cultural heritage sites.

name: Jordan Trail
description: 650-km hiking trail spanning Jordan from Umm Qais in the north to Aqaba in the south, passing through diverse landscapes, cultural sites, and local communities. One of the longest hiking trails in the Middle East.

name: Rainbow Street Amman
description: Vibrant cultural street in Jabal Amman featuring independent bookshops, art galleries, cafes, restaurants, and boutiques. One of Amman's most popular social and cultural destinations.

name: Abdoun Circle Area
description: Upscale entertainment and dining district in Abdoun, Amman. Features luxury restaurants, international cafes, high-end boutiques, and is one of Amman's premier social destinations.

name: Weibdeh Cultural District
description: Historic neighborhood in Amman known for its art galleries (including Darat Al Funun), cafes, and creative community. One of the city's artistic and bohemian quarters.

name: Jordan Ballooning
description: Hot air balloon experience over the Wadi Rum desert landscape. Offers a unique aerial perspective of Jordan's dramatic red desert scenery.

name: Al-Mothana Library
description: Well-known library and bookstore in Amman providing a quiet space for reading and studying with a collection including both Arabic and English books.

name: Umm Qais Resthouse
description: Resthouse and dining facility at the Umm Qais archaeological site with panoramic views of the Sea of Galilee, Golan Heights, and Jordan Valley. Managed by the RSCN.

name: Feynan Ecolodge
description: Award-winning eco-lodge in the Dana Biosphere Reserve operated by the RSCN. Solar-powered and candle-lit, offering a unique eco-tourism experience in one of the world's most sustainable hotels.

name: Beit Al-Anbat
description: Heritage site and cultural experience focused on Nabataean culture and history, offering insights into the civilization that built Petra.

name: Foresight Art Gallery (Foresight32)
description: The first private art gallery to exhibit art in Jordan, originally opened in 1991 as Baladna art gallery. Located at Sixth Circle in Amman. Showcases emerging and established local and international artists, and hosts cultural workshops, seminars, and training courses.

name: Regency Palace Amman
description: Five-star hotel in Shmeisani, Amman. A 20-floor hotel with 260 spacious rooms all with balconies. Features two swimming pools (heated indoor and rooftop), spa, fitness center, and home of Trader Vic's restaurant considered one of the best dining experiences in Amman.

name: Jordan Gate Towers
description: Twin skyscraper complex in Amman serving as a landmark mixed-use development with offices, hotel, and retail spaces. One of Jordan's most recognizable architectural landmarks.

name: Wadi Rum Rum Stars Camp
description: Eco-tourism camp in Wadi Rum offering desert camping experiences with Bedouin hospitality, stargazing, jeep tours, camel rides, and traditional meals in the protected desert area.

name: Jerash Chariot Race Show
description: Live chariot race and Roman soldier re-enactment show held at the Hippodrome in the Jerash archaeological site. One of Jordan's most popular tourist attractions showcasing ancient Roman entertainment.

name: Jordan Paintball
description: Paintball facility in Amman offering outdoor paintball experiences for groups, corporate team-building events, and birthday parties.

name: Amman Wave
description: Entertainment and activity center in Amman offering diverse leisure activities for families and young adults.

name: Dead Sea Museum
description: Museum near the Dead Sea shore documenting the geological, historical, and environmental significance of the Dead Sea and its surrounding region.

name: Jabal Al-Qal'a (Amman Citadel Hill)
description: The highest of Amman's seven hills, site of continuous human settlement for over 7,000 years. Now home to the Amman Citadel, Jordan Archaeological Museum, and panoramic views of Amman.

name: Amman Boulevard (Downtown)
description: Amman's historic downtown area (Al-Balad) combining traditional souks, street food, the Roman Theater, Odeon, and a mix of old and new cultural venues. A hub for local daily life and tourism.

name: Jordan Archery Club
description: Archery sports club in Jordan offering training and competitive archery. Affiliated with the Jordan Olympic Committee.

name: Black Iris Gallery
description: Art gallery in Amman showcasing Jordanian and regional contemporary art. Named after Jordan's national flower, the black iris.

name: Jordan Olympic Stadium
description: Multi-purpose stadium in Amman used for athletics and football events. Part of Jordan's national sports infrastructure.

name: Petra Moon Hotel
description: Four-star hotel in Wadi Musa, the gateway village to Petra. Offers comfortable accommodations for visitors exploring the Petra archaeological site.

name: Wadi Rum Night Luxury Camp
description: Luxury glamping camp in Wadi Rum offering private bubble tents, traditional Bedouin tents, and VIP suites with stunning desert views and stargazing experiences.

name: Jerash Forum Hotel
description: Hotel near the Jerash archaeological site offering accommodations for tourists visiting the ancient Roman city. Convenient base for exploring northern Jordan.

name: Aqaba International Market
description: Major shopping and commercial area in Aqaba offering a range of goods in a duty-free zone. Popular for electronics, perfumes, and consumer goods at competitive prices.

name: Amman Art Week
description: Annual arts week bringing together galleries, artists, and cultural institutions across Amman for a city-wide celebration of contemporary art and creativity.

name: Hussein Salt Football Stadium
description: Football stadium in Al-Salt, the capital of the Balqa Governorate. Home ground for Al-Salt SC and used for local football competitions.

name: Aqaba Beach Public Area
description: Public beach area in Aqaba on the Red Sea offering free access for swimming, water sports, and leisure. Popular with locals and budget travelers.

name: National Centre for Culture and Arts (NCCA)
description: Part of the King Hussein Foundation, the National Centre for Culture and Arts supports and promotes Jordanian cultural heritage, traditional crafts, and contemporary arts through programs and events.

name: Wild Jordan Center
description: RSCN's flagship eco-tourism center in Jabal Amman. Features a nature-themed cafe, organic food products, nature crafts shop, and serves as a booking center for eco-tourism in Jordan's nature reserves.

name: Petra Kitchen
description: Cooking school and cultural dining experience in Wadi Musa near Petra. Offers hands-on Jordanian cooking classes and traditional meals, allowing tourists to learn about local cuisine and culture.

name: Jordan Philatelic Society
description: Organization dedicated to philately (stamp collecting) in Jordan, promoting this hobby as a window into Jordanian history and culture.

name: Sela Zaman Restaurant & Heritage Village
description: Traditional Jordanian heritage village and restaurant in Amman offering authentic local cuisine in a recreated traditional setting. Popular with both locals and tourists for cultural dining.

name: Zarqa Cultural Center
description: Cultural center in Zarqa offering arts, music, theater, and educational programming for residents of Jordan's third-largest city.

name: Irbid Cultural Center
description: Cultural center in Irbid providing arts, cultural, and educational programming for northern Jordan's largest city.
